metaTitle: Copy Table as Image or Excel Data | {{BRAND_NAME}}
metaDescription: Copy a table as a PNG image to your clipboard — paste directly into any document, email, or presentation. Or copy as Excel data to paste into any spreadsheet.
heroHeadline: Copy your table. Paste it anywhere.
heroSubtext: Two copy modes: a rendered PNG image for documents and presentations, or tab-separated data for spreadsheets. One button, two destinations — no file download required.

benefits.heading: Copy as image — paste without exporting
benefits.body: The table is rendered at 2x resolution and written directly to your clipboard as a PNG. Paste into Word, Google Docs, email, Notion, Figma, or any tool that accepts images.

benefits.heading: Copy as Excel data — paste into any spreadsheet
benefits.body: Copies tab-separated values (TSV) to your clipboard — the exact format Excel and Google Sheets read as structured data. Paste and your table appears as a proper grid.

benefits.heading: No download, no file
benefits.body: Both modes write to your clipboard directly. No file is saved to your downloads folder. Paste immediately into whatever you are working in.

benefits.heading: Ctrl+C for Copy as image
benefits.body: Press Ctrl+C (Cmd+C on Mac) to copy the table as an image without opening the Copy menu. The quickest way to get your table into a document.

copyModes.name: Copy as Image
copyModes.description: Renders the table as a 2x PNG and writes it to the clipboard. Paste into any application that accepts images — Word, email, Notion, Figma, presentations.

copyModes.name: Copy Excel Data
copyModes.description: Copies tab-separated values (TSV) — the format Excel and Google Sheets use when pasting structured data. Open Excel, paste, your table appears as a grid.
copyModes.bestFor: Excel, Google Sheets, LibreOffice Calc, any spreadsheet

steps.heading: Click the Copy button
steps.body: Find the Copy button in the toolbar. Click the dropdown arrow to choose between Copy as Image and Copy Excel Data.

steps.heading: Choose your copy mode
steps.body: Copy as Image for documents and visual destinations. Copy Excel Data for spreadsheets and data tools. Or press Ctrl+C to copy as image directly.

steps.heading: Paste into your destination
steps.body: Switch to your target application and press Ctrl+V (Cmd+V on Mac). The table appears exactly as it was in Tablesmit — no reformatting, no cleanup.

useCases: Copying a pricing table as an image and pasting it directly into a client email
Pasting a structured data table from Tablesmit into Excel for further analysis
Inserting a research table as an image into a Word or Google Docs report
Pasting table data into Google Sheets to continue working in a spreadsheet

_codex_notes.note: copyModes is a new field specific to this page. Render the two modes as side-by-side cards in the hero section — before the benefits. Each card: name, shortcut badge (if exists), status badge ('Live' in green or 'Coming soon' in amber), description, bestFor. Copy as Image has status 'coming-soon' — render with the amber badge and a 'In the pipeline — Ctrl+C will trigger this' note.
